title: Shift absolute value graphs
description: y=|x-k|+h is the new equation of the absolute value function y=|x| shifted k units to the right and h units up. Practice this relationship between the graphical and algebraic representations of absolute value functions.
topic_path: Get ready for transformations of functions and modeling with functions

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is shifted up by $4$ units and to the right by $5$ units.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

In this case, we translate the graph horizontally by $\greenD{5}$ units and vertically by $\goldE{4}$ units.

$\begin{align}y&=|x-\greenD{5}|+\goldE{4}\end{align}$

In conclusion, the new equation is:

$y=|x-5|+4$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is shifted down by $6$ units and to the left by $9$ units.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

In this case, we translate the graph horizontally by $\greenD{-9}$ units and vertically by $\goldE{-6}$ units.

$\begin{align}y&=|x-\greenD{(-9)}|+\goldD{(-6)}
\\\\
&=|x + 9| - 6
\end{align}$

In conclusion, the new equation is:

$y=|x + 9| - 6$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is shifted down by $9$ units and to the right by $4$ units.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

In this case, we translate the graph horizontally by $\greenD{4}$ units and vertically by $\goldE{-9}$ units.

$\begin{align}y&=|x-\greenD{4}|+\goldD{(-9)}
\\\\
&=|x - 4| - 9
\end{align}$

In conclusion, the new equation is:

$y=|x - 4| - 9$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is shifted up by $7$ units and to the left by $8$ units.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

In this case, we translate the graph horizontally by $\greenD{-8}$ units and vertically by $\goldE{7}$ units.

$\begin{align}y&=|x-\greenD{(-8)}|+\goldD{7}
\\\\
&=|x + 8| + 7
\end{align}$

In conclusion, the new equation is:

$y=|x + 8| + 7$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is shifted to the left by $6$ units.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

In this case, we translate the graph horizontally by $\greenD{-6}$ units and vertically by $\goldE{0}$ units.

$\begin{align}y&=|x-\greenD{(-6)}|+\goldD{0}
\\\\
&=|x + 6| 
\end{align}$

In conclusion, the new equation is:

$y=|x + 6|$

assessment_items.question_content:
The graph of $y=|x|$ is shifted down by $9$ units.

**What is the equation of the new graph?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

In this case, we translate the graph horizontally by $\greenD{0}$ units and vertically by $\goldE{-9}$ units.

$\begin{align}y&=|x-\greenD{0}|+\goldD{(-9)}
\\\\
&=|x| - 9
\end{align}$

In conclusion, the new equation is:

$y=|x| - 9$

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}
f(x)&=|x|
\\\\
g(x)&=|x+2|+4
\end{align}$

We can think of $g$ as a translated (shifted) version of $f$.

**Complete the description of the transformation.**  
*Use nonnegative numbers.*

To get the function $g$, shift $f$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] by [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units and to the [[☃ dropdown 2]] by [[☃ numeric-input 2]] units.
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

$g(x)=|x-(\greenD{-2})|+\goldE{4}$

Since the function is translated right by $\greenD{-2}$, that means it is translated **left** by $2$ units.

The function is translated **up** by $\goldE{4}$ units.
To get the function $g$, shift $f$ *up* by $4$ units and to the *left* by $2$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}
f(x)&=|x|
\\\\
g(x)&=|x + 9| - 5
\end{align}$

We can think of $g$ as a translated (shifted) version of $f$.

**Complete the description of the transformation.**  
*Use nonnegative numbers.*

To get the function $g$, shift $f$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] by [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units and to the [[☃ dropdown 2]] by [[☃ numeric-input 2]] units.
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

$g(x)=|x-\greenD{(-9)}|+\goldD{(-5)}$

Since the function is translated right by $\greenD{-9}$, that means it is translated **left** by $9$ units.

Since the function is translated up by $\goldE{-5}$, that means it is translated **down** by $5$ units.
To get the function $g$, shift $f$ *down* by $5$ units and to the *left* by $9$ units.

To get the function $g$, shift $f$*left* by $2$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
$\begin{align}
f(x)&=|x|
\\\\
g(x)&=|x - 4| - 4
\end{align}$

We can think of $g$ as a translated (shifted) version of $f$.

**Complete the description of the transformation.**  
*Use nonnegative numbers.*

To get the function $g$, shift $f$ [[☃ dropdown 1]] by [[☃ numeric-input 1]] units and to the [[☃ dropdown 2]] by [[☃ numeric-input 2]] units.
hints:

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

$g(x)=|x-\greenD{4}|+\goldD{(-4)}$

The function is translated **right** by $\greenD{4}$ units.

Since the function is translated up by $\goldE{-4}$, that means it is translated **down** by $4$ units.
To get the function $g$, shift $f$ *down* by $4$ units and to the *right* by $4$ units.

assessment_items.question_content:
Function $g$ can be thought of as a translated (shifted) version of $f(x)=|x|$.



[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the equation for $g(x)$ ?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
The equation that results from translating the graph of the absolute value function $y=|x|$ to the right by $\greenD{h}$ units and up by $\goldE{k}$ units is:

$y=|x-\greenD{h}|+\goldE{k}$

Note that negative values of $\greenD{h}$ represent leftward shifts and negative values for $\goldE{k}$ represent downward shifts.

To translate the graph of $\blueD{f}$ to the graph of $\maroonD{g}$, we shift right $3$ units and down $1$ unit. So $\greenD{h}=\greenD{3}$ and $\goldE{k}=\goldE{-1}$.

$y=|x-\greenD{3}|+\goldE{(-1)}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

In conclusion:

$g(x)=|x-3|-1$

options.alt: A coordinate plane. The x- and y-axes both scale by one. The graph of f is V-shaped with a minimum at zero, zero. On the left side of the minimum is a ray with a slope of negative one, and on the right side is a ray with a slope of one. The graph of g is V-shaped with a minimum at three, negative one. On the left side of the minimum is a ray with a slope of negative one, and on the right side is a ray with a slope of one.
